front.title: Support for nothing-new notifications in the DNS

abstract.t: The DNS protocol has increasingly needed to carry larger records than it was originally designed to carry. This has resulted in performance impacts due to both the size increases and requiring TCP instead of only UDP. Of particular note is the expected large increase in records relating to post-quantum signing algorithms. To help mitigate, but not entirely prevent, these impacts, this document proposes a new "nothing new" NN flag, a LARGE Redirection Resource record type, and how these can integrate with current and future DNSSEC DNSKEY and RRSIG records.
